summary: End-to-end testing of Sprout (smart-fridge) MVP. Backend: 24/24 pytest tests passing across health, inventory CRUD, scan + commit, recipes (filters, detail, match scoring), grocery (CRUD, auto-replenish, from-recipe), family (get/patch members & household), alerts and stats. Frontend: app loads cleanly on mobile viewport; dashboard renders 4 stat cards + alerts card + featured recipe + 3 quick-action links; bottom nav routes correctly; inventory list renders 16 seeded items with category filters and search working; scan dialog opens and detection results render; recipes list with filters, detail dialog (ingredients with have-flag + steps) and add-to-grocery work; grocery list with add/toggle/auto-replenish works; family page renders household name, members and staples. Two integration glitches observed during automated UI testing (both relate to Emergent preview badge intercepting clicks at the bottom-right of the viewport).

ui_bugs.issue: Emergent preview badge (fixed bottom-right) overlays/intercepts pointer events on the 'Add item' / 'Save changes' submit button (data-testid=item-save). Form submits correctly via keyboard Enter, and a real desktop user can click it because the badge is small, but on mobile widths (390px) the badge sits visually on top of the submit button. Recommend raising dialog z-index above any external badge layer or adding bottom-padding to the dialog's footer so the submit button is positioned above the badge.

Same overlay issue: 'Add to fridge' button (data-testid=scan-commit) is intercepted by the Emergent preview badge at viewport bottom. Triggering by keyboard works; click does not. Add bottom safe-area / increase dialog z-index.

issues: Sticky footer with primary action sits too close to the viewport bottom on mobile; nothing reserves space for OS gesture / external floating badges. Add pb-6 / safe-area-inset-bottom.

data_testid_mismatches_vs_review_request.note: The review request enumerated expected data-testids that do NOT match the implementation. Implementation is internally consistent but main agent should reconcile naming (or update PRD). Mismatches found:

action_items: Raise dialog z-index (currently z-50) above any external embed badges, or add bottom safe-area padding to ItemDialog and CameraScan footers so the primary submit button is not occluded by the Emergent preview badge on mobile widths.
Optional: rename data-testids to match the review-request spec (or update spec) for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: GET /recipes computes ingredient availability using exact case-insensitive name match. This is brittle (e.g., 'Tortillas' in recipes is never in inventory). Acceptable for MVP but consider normalising/aliasing for accuracy of match_score.

server.py: /grocery/auto-replenish uses item['low_threshold'] as the grocery quantity. If a staple's low_threshold is 1 but actual low_stock condition is quantity <= low_threshold, you might want max(low_threshold, low_threshold*2) so the user is prompted to buy more than the minimum.
server.py: on_event('startup') is deprecated in newer FastAPI; consider lifespan handler.
Inventory.jsx: Search is case-insensitive on name only; no match against emoji/category. Minor.
ItemDialog/CameraScan: dialog z-index z-50 not sufficient to cover external floating widgets; consider z-[60] and pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)].
Recipes.jsx: filter-kid / filter-expiring testids should arguably be toggle-* to match spec, but functionally fine.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (all flows render and respond; 2 click-overlay glitches in dialog submit area due to preview badge)

test_credentials: none - app is fully public, no auth
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 16 inventory items, 10 recipes, 1 household with 4 members, 3 predicted grocery items on startup if collections are empty. Confirmed seeding works.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers all endpoints. Frontend UI is fully wired, but to drive the ItemDialog/Scan dialog submit buttons via Playwright on a 390px viewport the Emergent preview badge intercepts pointer events at the bottom-right; either hide it via CSS in test setup ('[id*=emergent]{display:none}') or submit via keyboard Enter. Data-testid naming in code uses inv-item-{id}, scan-commit, item-name/item-save, recipe-{id}, recipe-add-grocery, btn-auto-replenish, btn-clear-checked, household-name, member-{id} (not the names listed in the review request).
rca_of_dialog_click_issue: The Sonner toaster + the Emergent preview floating badge sit at the bottom of the viewport at a high z-index. The ItemDialog/CameraScan modal footers stick to the bottom of the dialog with no extra padding, so the primary submit button visually overlaps the badge area on a 390px viewport. Playwright's pointer-based click hits the badge instead of the submit button. Mitigation: add `pb-[calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom)+24px)]` to dialog footers OR raise dialog wrapper to `z-[60]` so it's above the badge.
